About Me

Bridging design
practice & pedagogy

I'm a UX Designer and Design Educator with over 3 years of experience in Communication Design, UX Research, and Academic Leadership. Currently serving as Course Leader & Assistant Professor of Design at Mody University of Science and Technology, I'm also pursuing a Ph.D. focused on the intersection of UX, Emerging Technologies, and Design Education.

My practice lives at the edge of what design can do — from building intuitive digital products to shaping curricula that prepare future-ready designers. I believe the best design is rigorous, human, and a little bit brave.

UX Research Wireframing Prototyping Figma Adobe XD Framer Design Systems Usability Testing AI Tools Curriculum Design Power BI Instructional Design

Design Education

Teaching as a
design practice

Courses designed around real-world briefs, AI-integrated workflows, and deep research thinking — preparing designers who are both rigorous and creative.

01

UX Design & Human-Machine Interaction

Bridging cognitive psychology, interface design, and emerging technology to shape intuitive experiences across touchpoints.

02

AI-Integrated Design Workflows

Hands-on coursework using Figma AI, Adobe Firefly, Framer, and generative tools to accelerate ideation and prototyping.

03

Communication & Graphic Design

Visual language, typography, and brand systems — from foundational principles to production-ready design outcomes.

04

Design Thinking Workshops

Facilitating cross-disciplinary innovation sessions with engineering and business faculties, translating empathy into actionable design strategies.

05

Design Systems & Instructional Design

Building scalable, consistent design languages and developing curriculum frameworks that bridge academia with industry practice.

06

Research Methods in Design

Ethnography, contextual inquiry, affinity mapping, and UX mapping — turning observations into design insights that matter.

↳ Student Work Highlights

Usability Testing on AI Tools

A comprehensive 4-month study testing ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Claude, and Blackbox across 9 participants — novice to expert. Structured tasks, data collection, and actionable UX recommendations.

Dakshita Sharma · UI/UX 3rd Year · Enrollment 230128

Trends & Technology — AI Ad Craft

An exploration of AI-powered creative workflows from pre-production to post-production, using prompting techniques like Chain of Thought, Tree of Thought, and Self-Refine prompting to create a full PUMA advertisement.

Divyanshi Panwar · UX/UI 2nd Year · Enrollment 240477
Design Process

How I
think through design

A research-led, iterative approach informed by 3+ years of professional practice and academic inquiry into human-centered design.

01
Discover

Ethnographic research, contextual inquiry, surveys, and stakeholder interviews to uncover real needs beneath stated desires.

02
Define

Affinity mapping, user personas, journey maps, and problem framing — crystallising what actually needs to be solved.

03
Ideate

Sketching, wireframing, and design thinking workshops. Divergent before convergent — quantity of ideas builds quality of solutions.

04
Prototype

From lo-fi sketches to high-fidelity interactive prototypes in Figma, Adobe XD, and Framer, built for testing not just showcasing.

05
Test & Learn

Moderated usability testing, task analysis, and satisfaction metrics — closing the loop and driving meaningful iteration.

UX Design · Crisis Tech

SafeAbroad: Crisis Playbook

Overview

India has over 18.5 million citizens living abroad and sends 760,000+ students overseas every year. When crises erupt — war, civil unrest, visa policy changes, natural disasters — these citizens face a moment of acute confusion with no clear system to help them.

SafeAbroad is a high-trust crisis companion app designed specifically for Indian citizens outside India. It provides clear, actionable, plain-language protocols during moments of acute stress — ensuring every citizen knows exactly what to do and how to reach support.

Problem
" No student should have to learn about Operation Ganga from a WhatsApp forward at 2am. That moment of panic — that's exactly what we're designing against.
User Research
Riya, 22
Medical student in Ukraine
"I kept refreshing the MEA website but couldn't find what I was supposed to do first."
Anjali, 28
Software engineer in the US on OPT
"There's so much noise, I couldn't find one clear answer about what Indians specifically should do."
Mohammed, 34
Construction worker in Kuwait
"We didn't even know the Indian embassy number. The employer never told us anything."
Vikram's Parents, 58
Tourists in Thailand
"We didn't want to trouble Vikram in India but we genuinely didn't know what to do."
Core Design Principles
01
Clarity over Jargon — Direct, step-by-step action plans replacing government-speak
02
Visual Trust & Calm — Cinematic navy and celestial theme for quiet authority
03
Heritage-Forward Identity — Diverse Indian scripts in the visual language
04
One-Tap Accessibility — Critical contacts and SOS always one tap away
UI Design
Key Features Designed
🆘 One-tap SOS with MEA Global Helpline
🏛️ Embassy locator by city/country
📋 Crisis Companion — Conflict, Civil Unrest, Visa, Disaster protocols
📡 Real-time social media alert integration (@MEAIndia)
🌐 Multilingual support for Indian diaspora languages
📥 Offline-first protocol downloads for low-connectivity zones
